{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"two consecutive prior attempts were blocked by pre-existing, unrelated failures in the full pre-commit/test gate rather than by this bead's own diff, and the specific failing symptoms differed between attempts: The bead itself is well-specified: PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE cite exact file:line locations (cli/internal/bead/factory.go:3-7, watcher.go:40-41, backend.go:144-147), the PROPOSED FIX names the concrete constructor to add (NewLifecycleSubscriber) and its target package, and the AC is numbered with two named Test functions plus a scoped go test command. Both prior attempts confirm the code change itself is correct and that the bead-scoped tests pass (`go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/...`). Both attempts stalled at AC #5 (`lefthook run pre-commit` passes) due to pre-existing failures unrelated to this bead's diff — first in cli/internal/server test suite (TestGraphQLBeadLookupReadsExternalCLIUpdateOnNextRequest, TestLandCoordinatorMetrics, etc.), then a second time with different symptoms (doctor flag redefined: unjam, fatal: not a git repository). This is a hidden external blocker outside this bead's NON-SCOPE-defined boundaries (fixing the server suite or execution environment is not part of this task), and it has now recurred twice with different concrete failures, indicating unstable shared gate state rather than an authoring defect. Implementation attempts cannot land until that external gate is fixed or the bead is temporarily waived past it by an operator.","fingerprint":"2e0258da7fd9abca6a40101b1c857203a9f3a137ed4d0ecc7619928f5a4f3e02","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"2e0258da7fd9abca6a40101b1c857203a9f3a137ed4d0ecc7619928f5a4f3e02"}

status=open
reason=Bash tool is unavailable in this execution environment — every invocation (verified via direct calls and via a fresh general-purpose subagent) fails immediately with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-eca2d238-20260708T145014-04b024a7'` before any command runs. This is a host/session infrastructure fault (tmp mount full or out of inodes for the claude-501 session dir), not a code or bead-authoring issue. Read/Edit/Write tools work fine, but there is no way to run `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/... ./internal/server/...`, `go build`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, or `git commit` without Bash. Per the execute-bead contract I am not committing untested/unverified code. This should be retried once the execution host's /tmp has space (or on a fresh worktree/host); no code changes have been made in this attempt.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"system_unready: Bead is well-specified: file:line root cause (cli/internal/bead/factory.go:3-7, watcher.go:40-41, backend.go:144-147), governing artifact TD-027 §21, explicit proposed fix, named parent (ddx-74452926), and named Test* functions in AC. Three prior attempts all ended no_changes, but the first (ENOSPC on /tmp mkdir) is a host infra fault unrelated to the bead's content and should not count as a content blocker. The second and third attempts independently (different harnesses/models) got the code changes passing for the bead's own packages but were blocked at lefthook/go-test on pre-existing, unrelated internal/server failures (doctor flag redefined: unjam, fatal: not a git repository, TestGraphQLBeadLookupReadsExternalCLIUpdateOnNextRequest, TestLandCoordinatorMetrics, TestLandCoordinatorMetricsRegistry, TestWorker_RequiredGatePass_LandsViaCoordinator, TestWorkerReport_DistinguishesCandidateAndLandedRev). That is a baseline gate problem, not a defect in this bead's scope. A narrow AC rewrite that scopes the test/lefthook gate to the bead's own packages plus a build-only check for internal/server callers, and excludes the known-broken go-test lefthook hook, removes the recurring external blocker while preserving all original intent, file:line evidence, named tests, non-scope items, and parent/spec references.","fingerprint":"13ca943771760cab41f1f6004cb1497dc8097a46c2a3b4d3f2f7c861c3b9de62","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"13ca943771760cab41f1f6004cb1497dc8097a46c2a3b4d3f2f7c861c3b9de62"}
